page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.4xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.u1 server family. Built on the KVM hypervisor, this instance features 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier across multiple workloads. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1851.99 and a multi-core score of 15444.6, complemented by a Geekbench multi-core score of 8709.0. Memory subsystem performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 847.52 GB/sec, which drops to 99.57 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write bandwidth of 57.38 GB/sec.

For application-level workloads, the instance demonstrates solid capabilities in database operations and text processing, achieving 15.56 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a Passmark database operations score of 7964.47. It also supports static web serving at a throughput of 16.54 GB/sec. While it can handle small-scale language model inference, processing 1266.34 tokens per second on a 135M model, performance drops significantly to 32.12 tokens per second for a 7B model, and it lacks the capacity for 70B models. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and caching layers where local storage is not a primary requirement.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, it offers a high 8.0 GB per core memory allocation, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate balanced performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8709.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 19492.46. Memory bandwidth reaches 847.52 GB/sec for cached small blocks, supporting high-throughput operations. The instance is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, including database hosting, Redis caching, and web serving, offering a cost-efficient profile for applications requiring substantial memory capacity without the overhead of local NVMe storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 128 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m8.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 128 GB RAM on a KVM hypervisor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring sixteen dedicated Intel Xeon cores and a high memory-to-core ratio for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
128.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB per core memory density
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on KVM hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for database hosting, Redis caching, and memory-intensive workloads
